page: Amazon Web Services r8idb.large is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volume storage, offering low-latency local storage directly attached to the hypervisor. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe SSD storage, the r8idb.large is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require fast local scratch space or caching. This hardware profile is suitable for small-scale databases, in-memory caches, and real-time data processing applications. The dedicated CPU allocation and Nitro hypervisor ensure consistent performance, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound tasks without the overhead of a GPU.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idb.large is a memory-optimized, x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel processor. It provides 2 dedicated vCPUs across 1 physical core, 16.0 GB of system memory, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU and fast local storage, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from low-latency local scratch space. It is well-suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-bound data processing tasks where dedicated CPU allocation and consistent resource density are required.
